name: Mint Chocolate Rusks
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Remove dividers from two 11 x 4 x 1 1/2-inch metal ice cube trays; butter and flour the trays. Sift together the flours, cocoa, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Toss chocolate chips with 2 teaspoons of the sifted mixture.
Cream butter in a large bowl on low speed for 5 minutes. Add sugar and continue beating for 3 to 4 minutes. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Blend in milk, vanilla extract, and melted chocolate. Blend in the sifted ingredients on low speed. Stir in chocolate chips.
Scrape batter into prepared pans, dividing evenly between them. Bake for 25 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool cakes in pan on rack for 3 to 4 minutes, then invert onto another rack and turn right side up. Cool for 10 to 15 minutes.
Cut each cake diagonally into about twelve 3/4-inch thick slices using a sharp serrated knife. Arrange slices on two jelly roll pans or cookie sheets. Bake slices for 7 to 9 minutes, turning them over and continuing baking for 7 to 9 more minutes, or until firm. Remove rusks to a cooling rack with a wide metal spatula.
If finishing with granulated sugar, sprinkle some while warm. Cool completely. If using chocolate glaze, dust off crumbs with a soft pastry brush.
Dip one end of a rusk into glaze and hold until it stops dripping, gently rocking the cookie from side to side. Place each rusk on parchment paper when chocolate is set (about 20 minutes). For extra decoration, dip glazed tip in chocolate sprinkles just before placing on parchment. Peel away cookies; store in an airtight container.
